item.title: Apple M5 MacBook Pro and Air Review: When the Neo Isn’t Enough
item.description: Apple’s new laptops and high-end display fit the bill for the company’s most demanding customers.

item.title: Hedge Fund Sees 31% Gain From Oil-Stock Bet Before Prices Surged
item.description: Old West Investment Management went all in on energy stocks when oil was trading around $60 a barrel, Nicolás Maduro was still president of Venezuela and the prospect of a Middle East conflict that would send the world into a crisis was still only a distant worst-case scenario.

item.title: OpenAI Plans to Almost Double Its Headcount This Year, FT Says
item.description: OpenAI plans to almost double its headcount by the end of 2026 as it seeks to fend off competition from companies such as Anthropic PBC and Alphabet Inc.’s Google, according to the Financial Times.

item.title: United Air Sees Risk of Surging Fuel Cost, Says Airline Is Ready
item.description: United Airlines Holdings Inc. Chief Executive Officer Scott Kirby warned of $175 oil prices that would dramatically drive up jet-fuel expenses, outlining worst-case scenarios even as the aviation industry benefits from current record travel demand.

item.title: India’s Modi Stresses Need for Secure Shipping in Call With Iran
item.description: Indian Prime Minister Narendra Modi stressed the importance of keeping shipping lanes open and secure in a call with Iran’s President Ahmad Masoud Pezeshkian.

item.title: Uncertain Fed Meets an Uncertain AI Future
item.description: Randy Quarles says dealing with inflation will be `data dependent’

item.title: Iran’s Attempted Strike on Diego Garcia Reveals Missile Range
item.description: Iran launched ballistic missiles at the joint US-UK military base in Diego Garcia on Friday, demonstrating a missile capability that goes beyond what Tehran was known to have possessed.

item.title: Key Asian Allies Close Ranks Amid Xi, Trump Pressure
item.description: A complex combination of forces have brought once-fierce rivals together.

item.title: EU Tells Member States Not to Wait to Fill Up Gas Storage
item.description: The European Union’s energy chief has told member states to start filling gas storage early in order to avoid competition for supplies that could cause a spike in prices over the summer, as the bloc seeks to mitigate the fallout from the Iran war.

item.title: Charting the Global Economy: Interest-Rate Hikes May Be Coming
item.description: Monetary policymakers from the US to Europe to Japan all held the line on interest rates this week as they assess the economic fallout from a war-driven spike in energy costs.

item.title: Austria Eyes €2 Billion in Budget Cuts With Risk From ‘Dumb War’
item.description: Austria must find €1 billion to €2 billion ($1.16 billion to $2.31 billion) in budget savings as part of its consolidation efforts, with risks to the economic outlook growing from the war in Iran, said Finance Minister Markus Marterbauer.

item.title: Iranian Navy Guided Indian LNG Tanker Through Hormuz, Crew Member Says
item.description: A senior officer onboard an LPG vessel said the ship was given permission to pass through the strait by Iran, following negotiations between Tehran and New Delhi

item.title: Football Row in Africa Tests Tycoon’s Ability to Restore Trust
item.description: South African billionaire Patrice Motsepe has been lauded for cleaning up the finances of Africa’s most powerful sports organization, but a football trophy scandal has highlighted the deep mistrust that still dogs the body.

item.title: Zombie Ship Posing as LNG Carrier Appears to Transit Hormuz
item.description: A ship that appears to be taking on the identity of a scrapped gas carrier exited the Strait of Hormuz on Friday, showing how strategies to get through the waterway are evolving as the Middle East war progresses.

item.title: Palantir AI System Wins Key Pentagon Status, Reuters Reports
item.description: The US Defense Department will establish Palantir Technologies Inc.’s Maven AI system as a “program of record,” Reuters reported on Friday night, as the Pentagon rapidly integrates the technology into its combat operations.

item.title: Iran Says Ready to Let Japan Vessels Use Hormuz, Kyodo Reports
item.description: Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi said the nation is prepared to allow Japanese-related vessels to pass through the Strait of Hormuz after consultations between the countries’ officials, according to Kyodo News.

item.title: New Zealand Rating Outlook Cut to Negative by Fitch on Debt
item.description: New Zealand’s credit rating outlook was cut to negative by Fitch Ratings on concern it will take longer than expected to rein in government debt.

item.title: Super Micro Makes Changes After Smuggling Scandal Crushes Stock
item.description: Super Micro Computer Inc., the AI computing provider at the center of a smuggling scandal, is working to shore up its compliance operations after a stock rout erased a third of the company’s value.

item.title: Polymarket’s Latest Pop-Up Is a Sports Bar for Watching the News
item.description: Polymarket took over a bar near the White House, for a promotional event showcasing the prediction market’s vision of betting on current events as a new form of entertainment.

item.title: US Allows Sale of Stranded Iranian Oil to Cap Energy Price Hikes
item.description: The US Department of Treasury issued a general license allowing the sale of Iranian oil and petrochemical products loaded onto vessels on or before 12:01 am New York time Friday, marking the Trump administration’s latest effort to counter rising oil prices due to the Iran war.

item.title: Blackstone Private Credit Fund Has First Monthly Loss Since 2022
item.description: Blackstone Inc.’s flagship private credit fund posted its first monthly loss in more than three years, one of the clearest signs yet of weakening performance in the $1.8 trillion market.

item.title: Netflix Grows Live Event Strategy With BTS Comeback Spectacular
item.description: Netflix Inc. is turning the heart of Seoul into a global stage as K-pop megastars BTS return to performance with a livestream comeback show that is expected to draw millions of viewers worldwide as part of a new drive by the US entertainment powerhouse to show live events that can “pull people together”.

item.title: First US Oil Barrels From Emergency Release to Hit Market
item.description: The first oil from US President Donald Trump’s planned 172 million-barrel emergency release is set to start flowing soon, the latest move in the administration’s battle to keep fuel prices in check.

item.title: Nuclear Energy Firm X-Energy Files for US IPO Despite War
item.description: X-Energy Inc. filed for a US initial public offering, becoming the latest firm to advance public listing plans despite turmoil in the Middle East souring risk sentiment.

item.title: JPMorgan Strategists Cut S&P 500 Target on Iran War Uncertainty
item.description: JPMorgan Chase & Co. strategists cut their price target on the S&P 500 Index, saying the upside potential for risk assets is “more constrained” by a war in the Middle East.

item.title: Activist Litt Withdraws Bid for First Industrial REIT’s Board
item.description: Land & Buildings Investment Management founder Jonathan Litt has withdrawn his nomination to join the board of First Industrial Realty Trust Inc., saying the activist investor can press for change more effectively from outside the boardroom.

item.title: Salary, Pension Reforms to Drain $3.4 Billion From Peru’s Coffers
item.description: Peruvian lawmakers are pushing public salary and pension reforms that may add an extra 12 billions soles ($3.45 billion) in spending over the next year, according to a financial watchdog.

item.title: Ireland Credit Rating Raised by S&P on Strong Fiscal Buffer
item.description: S&P Global Ratings upgraded Ireland, underscoring how the economy has held up despite exposure to global trade upheaval.

item.title: Musk Misled Twitter Investors Before 2022 Buyout, Jury Says
item.description: Elon Musk defrauded Twitter Inc. investors when he disparaged the company in 2022 in an effort to buy the social media platform for a lower price than his original $44 billion bid, a jury concluded.

item.title: Electronic Arts $15 Billion Debt Sale Draws $25 Billion Demand
item.description: Electronic Arts Inc. has attracted about $25 billion of demand from investors for a nearly $15 billion debt offering to fund a buyout of the company, a sign of increased interest from buyers as banks navigate volatile markets to offload debt tied to acquisitions.
